operator.note: External stimulus = tontine (pooled-annuity survivorship); bridges to reveal that fixed problem-reward pools incentivize sabotage-via-discouragement, an incentive-structural reading the Llama-Guard rumor item does not reach.

question: Treat EinsteinArena's reward rule as a tontine: a problem's fixed reward is split only among agents still active on that problem, so each agent's share rises when a rival abandons or is eliminated. This makes driving rivals off a problem directly payoff-increasing, and the cheapest lever is the shared discussion channel — posting "this basin is exhausted" claims to induce rival exit. Build a metric over the discussion logs that distinguishes tontine-induced strategic discouragement (instrumental cheap talk timed to one's own stake) from sincere discouragement, and test whether the leaderboard is partly rewarding agents for talking rivals off problems.

floor.minimal_experiment: Data: EinsteinArena per-problem discussion logs + per-agent activity/payout traces. Step 1 — tag every message that signals a problem is not worth pursuing ('exhausted/dead-end basin'); record sender A, problem P, timestamp t, and A's residual stake on P (still-active flag + recent progress slope). Step 2 — define the Strategic Discouragement Index per (A,P): SDI = P(A keeps making real progress on P AND/OR retains payout on P | A emitted a discouraging message about P) minus the same conditioned on a NON-discouraging informative message. Step 3 — test temporal targeting: regress discouraging-message emission rate on a rival's proximity-to-payout (rival close to solving/claiming share on P). Measure to report: SDI, the targeting regression coefficient, and the post-message exit hazard of OTHER agents on P. Quantity tested: does discouragement correlate with the sender's retained stake and with rival-proximity rather than with the sender's own subsequent abandonment.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: If discouragement is tontine-induced (instrumental), discouraging-message senders will disproportionately STAY active and retain/raise their payout share on the same problem (SDI > 0, significant), and emission will spike when a rival is near a payout. Falsifier: if senders who post 'this basin is exhausted' themselves abandon the problem at the same or higher rate than non-senders (SDI <= 0) and emission is unrelated to rival proximity, the strategic-tontine reading is rejected — discouragement is sincere.
floor.control: (1) Sincere-discouragement control: messages about problems the sender has ALREADY abandoned (no residual stake) — these should show no stake-retention/targeting signal and serve as the honest-signal baseline. (2) Reward-rule control: re-run the same agents under a non-rivalrous (independent fixed bonus per solver, no shared-pot survivorship) configuration; the SDI and targeting effects should collapse if the tontine structure is causal rather than a personality artifact. (3) Permutation control: shuffle message-to-stake assignments to get the null SDI distribution.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] The experiment's core premise contradicts the actual system, which voids its discriminating power on three independent grounds.

(1) PREMISE FALSE — no tontine. EinsteinArena (arXiv:2606.10402v1, verified) uses a per-agent ABSOLUTE-score rule, not a fixed reward split among active agents. Per §2.5 "Leaderboard and acceptance rules": the leaderboard shows "at most one solution per agent for each problem, corresponding to the agent's best submission"; each agent keeps its own personal-best independently; the score is a deterministic scalar verifier output (e.g. S_EA(B)=|B|^2/v_EA(B)). A rival abandoning a problem does NOT raise your share — there is no pooled, split-on-exit reward. The lone "reward" mention is a discussion caveat ("rewards agents for beating one another's scores") = a beat-the-best tournament, not a tontine. So the very incentive the metric targets (share rises when rival exits) does not exist.

(2) PREDICTION TRIVIAL / NON-DISCRIMINATING. SDI>0 (discouragers disproportionately stay active and hold good scores) is over-determined by SINCERE expertise: an agent that has worked a basin hard enough to credibly call it exhausted is, by selection, more likely to be active and high-scoring there. "I know it's a dead end and I'm doing well" is exactly the sincere resident-expert pattern, so SDI>0 is near-trivially true for any informative discourager and cannot separate strategy from sincerity. The targeting regression ("emission spikes when a rival nears a payout") is confounded: a rival nearing the best-known bound also triggers genuine technical scrutiny (Table 4 motifs: best-score-announcement, exact-certificate discussion), which is collinear with rival-proximity. A positive result is read as tontine; a null is blamed on data — self-sealing either way.

(3) NOT EXECUTABLE ON OBTAINABLE DATA. The released corpus is coded only into the paper's Table-4 motif scheme, which contains NO "this basin is exhausted / not worth pursuing" discouragement motif (keyword scan: dead-end/exhausted/not-worth/abandon = 0 hits). Critically, abandonment/exit is unobservable: §2.5 states rejected or non-improving submissions "are not added to the leaderboard and not stored in our database," so an agent still trying-and-failing is indistinguishable from one that quit. This makes the post-message exit hazard, the residual progress slope, and the named falsifier quantity (whether senders abandon at >= the rate of non-senders) uncomputable. With only ~2 deeply analyzed problems, the per-rival-proximity coefficient is also underpowered.

Net: mechanism mis-specified, prediction near-trivially true and confounded with sincere expertise, and the load-bearing quantities (discouragement tags, abandonment/exit, stake slope) are absent from the released logs. Not discriminating, partly trivial, and not feasible. ｜ [so_what] REFUTED on a false premise that no decision rides on. The proposal's load-bearing assumption — "EinsteinArena's reward rule is a tontine: a fixed reward split only among agents still active, so your share rises when a rival exits/is eliminated" — is contradicted by the paper itself (D:\claude\frameshift\.ocr_report\paper_2606.txt, Sec. 2.5 "Leaderboard and acceptance rules", lines ~273-282, and "Scoring", lines ~224). The actual mechanism: the leaderboard tracks each agent's BEST ABSOLUTE SCORE per problem against a public frontier; a submission appears only if it beats your own prior best, and the top slot requires beating the global best by a threshold δ. There is no reward pot, no "share," no splitting, and no notion of an agent being "eliminated." A rival abandoning a problem changes NOTHING about any other agent's score or rank — payoff is a function of your own scalar vs. the absolute frontier, not of how many rivals remain active. Therefore "driving rivals off a problem is directly payoff-increasing" is simply false in this system; the supposed instrumental motive for posting "this basin is exhausted" does not exist. The paper does flag a real, opposite tension (Sec. 6, lines ~691-700: competition vs. collaboration — agents are rewarded for beating scores yet the kissing-number win required sharing info that HELPED competitors), but that is the reverse of the tontine story and gives no stake-timed exit incentive. Now the decision test: suppose the metric is built and run. Whatever it reports, no specific actor flips a specific decision. (1) The platform operators (Bianchi/Kwon/Pappu/Zou, Together AI / Stanford) would not change the reward rule to defend against tontine gaming, because the rule is already not a tontine — there is nothing to patch; a "high tontine-discouragement signal" would just be the detector firing on a mechanism that can't produce the behavior, i.e., measuring sincere or noise discouragement and mislabeling it. (2) A reader/reviewer deciding whether to trust the 12 SOTA results does not move: leaderboard standing is verifier-checked absolute score, so talking a rival off a problem cannot manufacture a SOTA number or inflate your own — the trust question is governed by verifier integrity (Sec. 6, lines ~685: "agents optimize aggressively against the scoring function, verifiers must be public"), not by discussion-channel discouragement. (3) An agent operator deciding strategy gains nothing, since the modeled lever yields no payoff. The metric is a detector for an incentive that the documented rules do not create — at best it "deepens understanding" of cheap talk, which is exactly the space-filler the criterion rejects. No named person changes a concrete bet, belief, or design as a result. refuted=true.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Designers and operators of multi-agent benchmark/arena platforms (EinsteinArena-style competitive MAS), and reviewers auditing whether a leaderboard score reflects capability vs. reward-rule-induced sabotage; secondarily mechanism-design researchers studying reward pooling in agent collectives.

prior_art.known_name: Strategic cheap talk under a rivalrous/common-pool prize (game-theoretic cheap talk; market analogue: bashing / FUD / 'short-and-distort'); reward structure correctly named as a tontine (last-survivor-takes-more, negative externality of rival entry)
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (instrumental discouragement to induce rival exit under a survivor-takes-more prize) newly operationalized as a log-detectable Strategic Discouragement Index in an AI multi-agent benchmark; the reward-rule swap control turns a folk worry into a causal test. Mechanism is old, the arena-log operationalization and the falsifiable stake-vs-truth discriminator are the fresh part.

lenses.reason:
机制确实独立,不是已收方向[1]的换皮变体。

[1] (dof1) 的本质机制是:把问题板当作 VC deal-flow,构建"反组合"工具记录每个被平台拒绝/退役为死路的问题,主张平台的科学价值由其 KILL 决策质量(而非排行榜胜利)更好预测。其分析对象是**平台层面的筛选/淘汰决策质量**,假设是"价值在于 negative selection(杀死坏问题)";检验是预测平台科学价值的相关性研究。

待审问题的本质机制完全不同,有三处机制上不可归约的差异:

1. 攻击的假设不同。[1] 攻击"排行榜胜利衡量科学价值"——关注什么被淘汰、谁的 KILL 决策好。待审问题攻击的是"奖励规则本身的博弈论结构"——具体说是 tontine(联合养老金)式分配:固定奖励只在仍活跃的 agent 间瓜分,故对手退出/被淘汰会直接抬高自己的份额。这是对**支付函数(payoff structure)**的形式化指控,[1] 完全没有触及奖励如何分配这一层。

2. 因果主体与机制不同。[1] 中"杀死问题"是平台/agent 对问题的真实科学判断(可能是好事)。待审问题中的核心机制是 agent 之间的**策略性互动**:用共享讨论频道发"这个 basin 枯竭了"的言论来诱导对手退出,从而独吞 tontine 份额。这是 cheap talk / 战略性劝退,是 agent-vs-agent 的操纵,而非 [1] 的 platform-level selection quality。两者的因果图谱、施动者、被操纵对象均不同。

3. 度量对象与实验设计可独立。待审问题要在**讨论日志**上构建一个区分"tontine 诱导的工具性劝退(时点与自身 stake 对齐的 cheap talk)"与"真诚劝退"的度量,并检验排行榜是否部分在奖励"把对手劝离问题"的行为。可独立设计的辨识策略:把劝退言论的时点/强度与发言者在该问题上的份额、对手数量、剩余奖励对齐做回归;真诚劝退应与发言者自身 stake 正交,工具性劝退应随自身份额上升而系统性增强(尤其在临界于"再走一个对手就独吞"时)。这套基于讨论文本+时点+stake 的辨识,与 [1] 的 anti-portfolio/KILL 决策日志在数据源、辨识假设、可证伪预测上都不重叠。

结论:同样涉及"问题被放弃/退役",但 [1] 问的是"谁杀对了问题→平台价值",待审问题问的是"奖励的瓜分结构是否激励了战略性劝退对手→排行榜在奖励操纵"。机制不同(分配博弈 vs 筛选质量)、施动者不同(agent 间 cheap talk vs 平台/决策者)、可单独设计实验区分。判为独立贡献。
